plan.description: A full-stack application allowing users to create, read, update, and delete to-do items using a Flask REST API and a vanilla JavaScript frontend.
plan.architecture: Client-Server architecture. The vanilla JS frontend communicates with the Flask backend via REST API calls. The backend uses SQLAlchemy to interact with an SQLite database.

files.purpose: Centralized configuration for the application, including database URI.
Defines the SQLAlchemy model for the TodoItem (id, content, completed).
The main Flask application file. Initializes the database, defines API routes (GET, POST, DELETE), and handles business logic.
The main HTML structure for the application, linking CSS and JS.
Basic styling for the to-do list interface.
Client-side logic. Handles fetching data from the Flask API, rendering the list, and managing user interactions.

build_steps: Setup Python environment and install dependencies.
Build the frontend structure and styling.
Implement client-side logic and connect to the API.

subtasks.name: Setup and Configuration
subtasks.description: Initialize the project structure and define global settings.

subtasks.name: Backend Database Layer
subtasks.description: Define the database model and initialize the database connection.

subtasks.name: Backend API Implementation
subtasks.description: Create the Flask application and implement CRUD endpoints for TodoItems.

subtasks.name: Frontend Structure and Styling
subtasks.description: Create the basic HTML layout and apply necessary CSS styling.

subtasks.name: Frontend Logic and Integration
subtasks.description: Implement JavaScript logic to interact with the Flask API, render the list, and handle user input.
